data.description:
## Why

Audit Reliability finding #1 (HIGH). Two clones can both claim the same open ticket (the write lock is per-clone). On merge the STATUS fork resolves deterministically (lower event UUID wins) and the assignee resolves by last-writer-wins HLC — a *different* key — but **both agents were told CLAIMED** and proceed to duplicate work. The resolved fork is only a replay-time logger.warning (PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED, reducer/_processors.py:159-165) surfaced by no user-facing command. There is also no test covering two-clone concurrent claim (audit finding 7.1): the claim storm test is single-clone and the two-clone doctrine test contains no claim.

## What

1. After the post-claim push, re-fetch and re-read the ticket; if the assignee is not the claimant (or the claimant's STATUS event lost the fork), exit 10 with a clear "claim lost on merge" message.
2. Surface resolved STATUS forks as an fsck/validate finding and a show annotation so a lost claim is discoverable after the fact.
3. Add the missing two-clone concurrent-claim regression test: both clones claim offline, push/merge both ways, assert converged assignee is deterministic and the loser detects the loss.

## Scope

In: claim path (_commands/txn.py, _commands/claim.py), reducer fork reporting (reducer/_processors.py), fsck/validate/show surfacing, tests/integration/test_concurrency_regression.py. Out: changing the fork-resolution rule itself (lower-UUID-wins stays; docs I8), distributed locking.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] A claim that loses the cross-clone race is reported to the loser (retroactive exit 10 path or an equivalent explicit signal) once the merge is visible locally
- [ ] PARENT_CHAIN_FORK_RESOLVED appears as an fsck or validate finding and as a show annotation on the affected ticket
- [ ] New two-clone concurrent-claim test in the doctrine suite: deterministic converged assignee + loser detection asserted
- [ ] Existing claim-storm and concurrency-regression suites stay green (make test)
- [ ] docs/concurrency.md updated to describe the surfacing behavior
data.title: Surface lost cross-clone claims: post-claim verification, fsck/validate/show fork surfacing, two-clone claim test
